text: Demand remained strong across enterprise customers. We expect continued growth next quarter. Supply chain constraints are gradually easing.
Inflation remains a concern. Global logistics costs remain elevated. Management believes demand trends remain healthy.
Customer retention continues to improve.
Enterprise adoption accelerated. Pricing pressure has moderated. We expect strong momentum in Q3.
Competitive pressure exists. Currency fluctuations may impact revenue. New product launches contributed positively.
Pipeline remains healthy.
Customer demand exceeded expectations. Supply chain bottlenecks largely resolved. International expansion continues.
Geopolitical uncertainty remains. Regulatory changes may affect operations. Strong customer growth across regions.
Profitability improved significantly.
Record quarter performance. Management expects continued growth. Demand remains exceptionally strong.
Macroeconomic slowdown remains possible. Talent acquisition costs continue rising. Revenue growth exceeded internal expectations.
Operating leverage improved.
Strong start to the fiscal year driven by enterprise renewals. Investments in automation are beginning to pay off. Seasonality impacted sequential expansion slightly, but YoY trends remain robust.
Increased cyber security threats require higher defensive spend. Labor market tightness may impact RD delivery timelines. Gross margin slightly contracted due to upfront infrastructure investments.
Operating cash flow remains healthy and aligned with projections.
New AIdriven product modules saw record adoption rates. North American markets outperformed our internal forecasts. We are raising our fullyear guidance based on current pipeline visibility.
Intense competition in the midmarket segment could squeeze pricing. Foreign exchange headwinds may create minor revenue drag. Expansion of gross margin driven by favorable product mix shifts.
Sales and marketing efficiency improved yearoveryear.
European expansion is yielding solid initial returns despite local economic headwinds. Customer churn hit an alltime low this quarter. Strategic partnerships are expanding our addressable market.

Evolving global data privacy laws could increase compliance costs. Macroeconomic softening in key European zones poses a growth risk. Operating margin expanded due to scale and cost containment strategies.
Deferred revenue balances grew substantially, indicating strong future bookings.

An exceptional finish to 2024, crossing historical revenue thresholds. Enterprise multiyear deals accelerated significantly in December. We enter 2025 with strong fundamental momentum and high visibility.

Potential shifts in corporate tax rates could impact net profitability. Heavy reliance on primary cloud infrastructure providers limits operational flexibility. Record annual performance driven by both new customer acquisition and expansion.
The board has approved an expansion of our share buyback program reflecting capital strength.
We carried the strong closing momentum of last year into Q1. Professional services revenue saw an unexpected upside. Implementation timelines for major cloud migrations have shortened.

Geopolitical trade restrictions could indirectly affect our hardware partners. Higher energy and cloud data center costs could pressure margins. Growth continues at a stable pace despite broader macroeconomic caution.
The balance sheet remains unleveraged with strong liquidity.
Our SaaS migration is officially complete, driving higher recurring revenue. Net expansion rate among existing clients remains above 120%. Operational efficiences are manifesting across all units.

Slowing down of tech spending in the financial sector might pose headwinds. Patent litigation costs could experience a minor uptick. Gross margins hit a record high due to softwareheavy delivery streams.
RD efficiency gains allowed us to reinvest in market expansion.
APAC region performance exceeded our threeyear growth roadmap. Enterprise tier clients are increasingly signing up for multiproduct bundles. We remain comfortable with current consensus expectations.

Volatile global currency markets could create translation headwind effects. Wage inflation in emerging tech hubs remains a point of focus. Operating profit increased significantly on a yearoveryear basis.
Working capital cycles optimized, yielding higher operating cash flow.
A monumental year for ABC, closing out 2025 with stellar financial health. Our nextgen platform has fully captured the market vanguard. Guidance for 2026 will reflect our expanding market share.

Aggressive counterpricing by legacy competitors entering our niche. Integration complexities from upcoming strategic MA maneuvers. Full year revenue crosscompared to 2023 demonstrates sustained compounding.
Strong net income margins reflect high operating leverage across the business model.
